description: Analyze the relationship between residential density and household vehicle ownership across San Francisco using the UrbanSim dataset in the notebook located at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/parking_reform_analysis.ipynb. Using the 'households', 'buildings', and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, generate a dataset aggregated by zone_id that includes household_count, avg_cars_per_hh, avg_income, and avg_building_units (for residential buildings). The analysis must filter for statistical significance by excluding zones with fewer than 50 households. Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient between building scale (avg_building_units) and car ownership (avg_cars_per_hh). Export the aggregated zone metrics to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_vehicle_metrics.csv (including columns: zone_id, household_count, avg_cars_per_hh, avg_income, avg_building_units) and the statistical results ('pearson_correlation' and 'num_zones_analyzed') to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/correlation.json. Create a bubble chart showing the relationship (X-axis: avg_building_units, Y-axis: avg_cars_per_hh, Bubble Size: household_count) and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/auto_ownership_chart.png. The final notebook must be saved with all cells executed.
